mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of triggered instances over a five-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and residential investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 55 percent of occurrences or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production levels reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0 percent annually, or 5-day forward returns show no significant alpha with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a 2.5 percent annualized growth rate, anticipated central bank tightening compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in greater than 65 percent of high-inflation trigger months over a ten-year sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls directly increase aggregate household income and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between payroll changes and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.05 in any rolling three-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns in greater than 50 percent of quarters or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent indicates a sudden contraction in factory output and supply chain bottlenecks, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of sharp industrial production declines over a seven-year backtest.
